headline: Bank of America Agrees to Pay $72.5 Million to Settle Epstein Lawsuit
summary: Bank of America  agreed to pay $72.5 million to settle a lawsuit that alleged the bank kept ties to people close to  Jeffrey Epstein  and failed to flag suspicious payments between the convicted sex offender and his associates and victims.  The proposed class-action lawsuit was filed last year on behalf of a woman who said she was sexually abused and trafficked by Epstein between 2011 and 2019, as well as on behalf of other victims.  The lawsuit alleged that Bank of America “financially benefited” from Epstein’s circle and failed to file suspicious activity reports with the government, despite the information it gleaned from the accounts it provided to those around him.

The proposed class-action lawsuit was filed last year on behalf of a woman who said she was sexually abused and trafficked by Epstein between 2011 and 2019, as well as on behalf of other victims.  The lawsuit alleged that Bank of America “financially benefited” from Epstein’s circle and failed to file suspicious activity reports with the government, despite the information it gleaned from the accounts it provided to those around him.

headline: Epstein victims to get $72.5M from Bank of America settlement
summary: The settlement by Bank of America comes nearly three years after JPMorgan Chase and Deutsche Bank settled similar lawsuits by victims of Jeffrey Epstein.

headline: The Iran War and Oil Prices Bring Risk of Deeper Market Lows. There Are Echoes of 2008.
summary: Stock markets are facing the stark reality of an even sharper pullback heading into the final trading days of the first quarter, as bond investors continue to worry over stagflation risks tied to the Iran war and oil prices indicate no imminent let up in pressure.  Bond markets, meanwhile, suggest there could be even more declines to come, with 2-year Treasury note yields having surged some 60 basis points since the war began and 10-year note yields adding 50 basis points.

headline: How The Investment Story For Bank Of America (BAC) Is Shifting On Wall Street
summary: Bank of America’s modeled fair value has been adjusted slightly, moving from US$62.19 to US$61.77, which keeps the change modest but still meaningful for anyone tracking the story closely. That trim lines up with recent Street research, where some firms are raising targets and others are cutting them as they revisit assumptions and sort into more bullish or more cautious camps. headline: Watson Links and Bank of America Partner to Expand Youth Golf Access and Mentorship through Golf with Us
summary: Watson Links, the nonprofit youth golf mentoring organization founded by eight-time major champion Tom Watson, today announced a new partnership with Bank of America as part of Golf with Us, which aims to make golf more accessible while strengthening communities through the power of sport.

headline: BofA’s Hartnett Says Consumer Stocks Best Play for War Aftermath
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Investors should buy consumer stocks in a way to play “policy panic” as the US strives to prevent a recession, according to Bank of America’s Michael Hartnett. headline: Bank of America Names 2 Top Neocloud Stocks to Buy in 2026
summary: Talk about cloud computing, and most conversations quickly circle back to the usual giants – the hyperscalers. Names like Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet tend to dominate the narrative, and for good reason. Their scale, infrastructure, and reach have long defined what the cloud looks like. But the hyperscalers are hardly the only game in town. The neoclouds, the array of smaller, specialist companies that provide cloud services at smaller scales, are building a growing niche. headline: How The Keysight Technologies (KEYS) Story Is Evolving With New Growth Drivers And Valuation Views
summary: Keysight Technologies is now being valued slightly higher in fresh research, with one updated fair value estimate moving from US$298.62 to US$301.54. That modest shift comes as analysts react to fiscal Q1 results, highlighting stronger orders, broader demand beyond AI, and raised long term earnings frameworks, while still debating how much optimism is already reflected in current targets. As you read on, you will see how these differing views are shaping the evolving narrative around Keysight...
